hadiths.en:
The Imam {a.s} said: 
"When a believer dies, the places on earth where they used to worship Allah {SWT}, the 
door through which their deeds would ascend, and their place of prostration weep for 
them." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 139 • Al-Wafi, Part 1, p. 148 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
187 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 4, p. 629 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 12, p. 131 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 381
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨١

hadiths.ar:
383  -  وَّ ذَّج َّلَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّلَّى ج َّذِي ح َّه َّ وَّ هِى َّ لِمَّا ن ِهَّا ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه ن  َّا مَّا ن َّزَّى ن ِك ِ ي َّا  
ج َّذِي ح َّه ُّ  ق َّا ِذ َّإ ق َّذِمْث ِ عَّلَّى ص َّزَّإن ِزِك ِ ق َّا َّق ْزِن ِي هِن  َّ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن ْن َّه ُّ عِمْزَّإن َّ وَّ كُّلْي ُّمُّ 
إ ُّج ْث ُّ مُّوسَّى وَّ إ سِي َّه ُّ إِمْزَّإ َّة ُّ ق ِزْعَّوْن .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Whoever considers tomorrow 
as part of their lifespan has not kept good company with death."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 139 • Tahaf al -Uqool, Part 1, p. 49 • Al -Majāzāt al-
Nabawiyyah, Part 1, p. 198 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 191 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 437 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 
74, p. 153 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 382
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٢

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} entered upon Khadijah {s.a} while she was 
experiencing what she {s.a} was enduring (her final illness).  
He {saws} said to her: "In spite of what we see happening to you, Khadijah {s.a}, when you 
meet your co-wives in the Hereafter, give them my greetings of peace."  
She {s.a} asked: "Who are they, O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}?"  
He {saws} replied: "Mary {s.a}, the daughter of Imran {a.s}, and Kulthum {s.a}, the sister of Musa 
{a.s}, and Asiya {s.a}, the wife of Pharaoh."  
She {s.a} responded: "With pleasure, O’ Messenger of Allah {saws}."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 570 • Tafsir al -Safi, Vol. 5, p. 199 • 
Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 19, p. 24 • Tafsir Nur Al -Thaqalayn, Part 5, p. 376 • Tafseer Kanz Al -Daqaiq, Part 13, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 383
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٣

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 384
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٤

hadiths.ar:
386   -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :   «
مَّاب َّ لَّهُّ مَّي ِّث   ن َّهَّارإً ق َّان ْي َّط َّزَّ ن ِهِ إَّلل َّي ْلَّ لأَّ ي َّن ْي َّط ِزُّوإ ن ِمَّوْي َّاكُّمْ طُّلُّوعَّ إَّلس  َّمْسِ وَّ لأَّ ع ُّزُّون َّهَّا ع ح ِّلُّوإ ن ِهِمْ إ ِلَّى مَّص َّ اج ِعِهِمْ 
ي َّزْجَّمُّكُّمُّ إَّلل َّه .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"The honor of the deceased is their hastening (to be buried)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 140 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 282 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, 
p. 474 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 385
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٥

hadiths.ar:
387   -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «
رَّب ِّ مَّا ن َّلَّغ َّ مِن ْ عِي َّاذَّة ِ إَّلْمَّزِي ض ِ مِن َّ إَّلْأ َّج ْزِ ق َّالَّ إ ُّوَّكِّلُّ ن ِهِ مَّلَّكاً ي َّعُّوذُّةُّ ق ِى  ق َّن ْزِةِ إ ِلَّى مَّحْس َّزِ ةِ ق َّالَّ ي َّا رَّب ِّ ق َّ مَّا لِمَّن ْ ع َّسَّلَّ  
إَّلْمَّوْي َّى ق َّالَّ إ َّع ْسِلُّهُّ مِن ْ ذ ُّن ُّون ِهِ كَّي َّوْمَّ وَّلَّذَّي ْهُّ إ ُّم ُّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: "I do not want to find among you a man whose 
deceased loved one died at night and he waits until morning, or a man whose 
deceased loved one died during the day and he waits until night. Do not wait for the 
rising or setting of the sun with your dead. Hurry them to their resting places; may Allah 
{SWT} have mercy on you." 
The people replied: "And may Allah {SWT} have mercy on you, O’ Messenger of Allah 
{saws}." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 137 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 140 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 1, p. 427 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 281 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 471 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 386
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٦

hadiths.ar:
388   -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إَّلْأ َّمَّان َّه َّ ع َّق َّزَّ إَّلل َّهُّ لَّه 
ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} said: "In what Musa ibn Imran {a.s} 
privately spoke to his Lord {AZJ}, the Mighty and Glorious,  
Musa {a.s} said: 'O’ Lord {AZJ}, what is the reward for visiting the sick?'  
Allah {SWT} replied: 'I will appoint an angel to visit them in their grave until the Day of 
Resurrection.'  
Musa {a.s} asked: 'O’ Lord {AZJ}, what about the one who washes the deceased?'  
Allah {SWT} replied: 'I will cleanse them of their sins as on the day their mother gave birth 
to them.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 387
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٧

hadiths.en:
The Imam {a.s} said: 
"Whoever washes a deceased believer and fulfils the trust in that act, Allah {SWT} will 
forgive them." It was asked: "How does one fulfil the trust in this matter?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "By not disclosing what they see and maintaining their duty until the 
deceased is buried." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 141 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 496 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 388
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٨

hadiths.ar:
390   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إ ِلأ َّ عَّق َّا إَّلل َّهُّ عَّن ْه .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Any believer who washes the 
body of another believer and says while turning it:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, this is the body of Your believing servant; You have taken their soul from 
it and separated them from each other, so forgive, forgive, forgive,'  
Allah {SWT} will forgive the washer's sins for a year, except for major sins." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 164 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 141 • Thawab al -A’mal wa 
Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 195 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 540 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 303 • Al-
Waezin, Vol. 2, p. 489 • Sulwat al-Hazin, Part 1, p. 253 • Falah Al-Sael, Part 1, p. 78 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 285 
• Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 494 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 389
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٨٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There is no believing servant who washes a deceased believer and says while washing 
them: 
'O’ Lord {AZJ}, Your forgiveness, Your forgiveness,' except that Allah {SWT} forgives them." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 164 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 141 • Falah Al-Sael, Part 1, 
p. 78 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 286 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 494 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 300 • Mustadrak 
Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 172 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 390
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٠

hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"The one who washes the deceased should be the closest person to them or the one 
whom the guardian (Wali) of the deceased appoints for this task."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 141 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 293 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, 
p. 535 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 391
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩١

hadiths.ar:
393  -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلص َّق  َّارُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ ن ْن ِ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  كَّمْ جَّذ ُّ إَّلْمَّاءِ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّع ْسَّلُّ 
ن ِهِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ُّ كَّمَّا رَّوَّوْإ إ َّن  َّ إَّلْح ُّن ُّث َّ ي َّع ْي َّسِلُّ ن ِسِي  َّه ِ إ َّرْطَّالٍ مِن ْ مَّاءٍ وَّ إَّلْحَّان ِض َّ ن ِي ِسْعَّه ِ إ َّرْطَّالٍ   ق َّهَّلْ لِلْمَّي ِّث ِ جَّذ   مِن َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ  
إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّع ْسَّلْ ن ِهِ ق َّوَّق  َّغَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّ م.   
وهذ إ إلي وق ي غ ق ى  ج مله  ي وق ي عاي ه عن ذى  ن ح طه علي ه إلسلام ق ى  صحي ق ه .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever washes a deceased person and conceals and keeps secret what they see, 
they will emerge from their sins like the day their mother gave birth to them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 141 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 496 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 392
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٢

hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn al -Hasan al-Saffar wrote to Imam Hasan ibn Ali Al -Askari {a.s} 
asking: 
"What is the prescribed amount of water for washing the deceased, as it has been 
narrated that the junub (one in a state of major ritual impurity) bathes with six ratls of 
water and the menstruating woman with nine ratls of water ? Is there a prescribed 
amount of water for washing the deceased?"  
And Imam {a.s} replied in his own handwriting, saying: "The prescribed washing of the 
deceased is to continue until they are purified, if Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, wills."  
[AL SADUQ] 
This response was among a series of his responses that I possess in his handwriting 
on a document. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 431 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
195 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 312 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 536 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 393
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٣

hadiths.ar: 395 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ ق ِى  جَّذِي ث ٍ إ ج َّز .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Water should not be heated for washing the deceased."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 322 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 
328 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 1, p. 208 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 498 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 499 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 295 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 394
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٤

hadiths.en:
It is also narrated in another hadith: "Except if it is a cold winter, in which case, 
you should protect the deceased as you would protect yourself."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 395
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٥

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Do not leave your deceased alone, for Shaytan may disturb them within their body."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 142 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 282 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, 
p. 466 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 396
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٦

hadiths.ar:
398   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ إ َّ ي َّصْلُّحُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْط ُّزَّ إ ِلَّى إِمْزَّإ َّي ِهِ جِي ن َّ ي َّمُّوب ُّ 
إ َّوْ ي َّع ْسِلُّهَّا إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ عِن ْذَّهَّا مَّن ْ ي َّع ْسِلُّهَّا وَّ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ هَّلْ ي َّن ْط ُّزُّ إ ِلَّى مِي ْلِ ذ َّلِك َّ مِن ْ ر َّوْج ِهَّا   جِي ن َّ ي َّمُّوب ُّ ق َّق َّال 
ن ِذ َّلِك َّ إ ِن  َّمَّ ا ي َّق ْعَّلُّ ذ َّلِك َّ إ َّهْلُّ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ كَّزَّإهِي َّه َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْط ُّزَّ ر َّوْج ُّهَّا إ ِلَّى س َّى ْءٍ ي َّكْزَّهُّون َّهُّ مِن ْهَّا .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} about 
washing the deceased in an open space.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no issue with it, but if they are covered with a curtain (for 
privacy), that is more beloved to me." 
[REFERENCES] Masa’il Ali ibn Ja’far, Part 1, p. 212 • Qurb al-Isnad, Part 1, p. 182 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 142 • 
Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 142 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 431 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 328 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 538 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 288 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 397
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٧

hadiths.ar:
399  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ق َّاطِمَّه َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ مَّن ْ ع َّسَّلَّهَّا ق َّق َّال مُّ 
لِأ َّن  َّهَّا كَّان َّث ْ صِذِّي ق َّه ً لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لِي َّع ْسِلَّهَّا إ ِلأ َّ صِذِّي ق   .
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about 
whether it is permissible for a man to look at his wife when She dies or to wash her if 
there is no one else to wash her, and likewise, if a woman can do the same for her 
husband when he dies. Imam {a.s} replied: 
"There is no issue with that. It is usually the woman's family who may dislike her 
husband seeing something he would dislike about her."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 157 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 
439 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 198 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 295 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 528 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 398
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٨

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about about who 
performed the washing (Ghusl) of (the body of) Sayyida Fatimah Al -Zahra {S.A}?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} performed 
her washing because She was a ‘Al -Siddiqah’ (the most truthful and righteous one) ; 
and her ghusl is not permitted except by a ‘Al-Siddiq’ (truthful and righteous person)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 23, H. 399
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٣، ح ٣٩٩

hadiths.ar:
وض ع الج رت دت ي ن  
401 - وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ عِل َّه ِ إَّلْح َّزِي ذَّة ِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Touching a deceased person at the time of their death and after their washing, and 
kissing them, is not an issue”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
If one's clothing comes into contact with the body of the deceased, then they must 
wash whatever part of the clothing was touched. 
When preparing the deceased, the person washing starts by preparing the shroud.  
They begin with a sheet (namat), spreading it out, then placing a "habrah" (a specific 
cloth) and sprinkling some powdered perfume (dharirah) on it.  
Next, they spread out the izar (waist cloth) on top of the habrah, sprinkling more of the 
powdered perfume, then place the shirt (Qamis) on top of the izar, again sprinkling the 
powdered perfume.  
They take two fresh, green palm branches (Jaridatayn), each the length of 
approximately a forearm, and if they are the length of an arm or a span, there is no 
issue.  
He writes on the Izar (wrap), Qamis (shirt), the habrah, and the two branches:  
"So-and-so bears witness that there is no god but Allah {SWT}."  
They then wrap all of it together. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 100 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 430 • Wasail 
Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 295 
PLACING THE JARIDATAYN  
֎ HADITH 401 – 407 ֍

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 400
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٠٠
